_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: FL House Orlando/Osceola delegation batch (state tier): Eskamani HD-42, Lopez HD-43, Harris HD-44, Spencer HD-45, Alvarez HD-46. Evidence from FL House/Senate bill histories & party-line tallies (SB300 6-week ban, HB1521 trans-bathroom, HB543 permitless carry, SB1718 immigration, HB759 gun-age, HB1205 petition restrictions), issue-group endorsements (Planned Parenthood PAC, Equality Florida, Moms Demand/Everytown, FLIC Votes), ChoiceTracker, and candidates' own campaign statements. TWO cross-party deviations captured as TRUE: Alvarez voted YES on HB759 (lone Dem lowering gun-purchase age 21->18) and Spencer's self-stated pro-police-funding advocacy. Cells with only party-inference or no record left null.

profile.background: Florida House District 42 (Orlando/Orange County), since 2018. Former Planned Parenthood Senior Director (Central/SW FL, 2012-2018). Term-limited from the FL House in 2026; filed to run for Orlando Mayor in 2027 (NOT governor).

anna-v-eskamani@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-42 (D), since 2018; term-limited 2026. [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive, well-documented record. Sanctity of life: former Planned Parenthood Senior Director; led the FL House Democratic Caucus against the 2023 6-week abortion ban (SB300); endorsed by Planned Parenthood PAC, NARAL, EMILY's List, Ruth's List. Marriage/sexuality: endorsed by Equality Florida, LGBTQ Victory Fund; filed a 2020 bill barring voucher dollars from schools that discriminate against LGBTQ students; publicly defended trans access to care and opposed the 2023 anti-trans bathroom bill. Education: vocal anti-voucher public-school advocate (removed from the PreK-12 subcommittee in 2020 for being too vocal for public schools). Immigration: publicly opposed SB1718 (2023). Guns: co-sponsored the 2023 assault-weapons + large-capacity-magazine ban (HB579/SB462), defended FL's red-flag law against repeal, endorsed by Everytown/Moms Demand/Brady/Giffords. No conservative cross-party deviation found. DATA-INTEGRITY FLAG (follow-up dedup, not handled here): a separate record 'anna-eskamani-gov' frames her as a 2026 Governor candidate -- that is STALE/FALSE (she is term-limited from the House in 2026 and running for Orlando Mayor in 2027); the two records should be deduped (survivor = this sitting HD-42 record).

0.note: Former Planned Parenthood Senior Director; led the FL House Democratic Caucus against the 2023 6-week abortion ban -- documented pro-choice, does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Opposite of abortion abolition: filed 50+ amendments to block the 2023 ban; career pro-choice advocate.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood PAC, NARAL, EMILY's List, and Ruth's List.

0.note: Endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC and LGBTQ Victory Fund; does not affirm man-woman-only marriage.

1.note: Pro-LGBTQ record; opposite of opposing same-sex marriage/civil unions.

2.note: Publicly defended transgender access to care and opposed the 2023 anti-trans bathroom bill.

4.note: Filed a 2020 bill barring public voucher dollars from schools that discriminate against LGBTQ+ students/parents -- promotes, not opposes, LGBTQ protections in policy.

0.note: Vocal opponent of school-voucher expansion; removed from the PreK-12 Quality Subcommittee in 2020 for being 'too vocal' for public schools.

3.note: Opposed GOP voting-restriction legislation; pro-mail-in / pro-ballot-access posture (opposite of opposing mass mail-in/drop boxes).

2.note: Publicly opposed SB1718 (2023); pro-immigrant posture opposite of anti-sanctuary enforcement.

3.note: Opposed SB1718, which included E-Verify mandates.

0.note: Endorsed by Everytown, Moms Demand, Brady, Giffords -- opposite of supporting constitutional carry.

1.note: Co-sponsored the 2023 assault-weapons + large-capacity-magazine ban (HB579/SB462); defended FL's red-flag law against repeal.

profile.background: Florida House District 43 (Orange County/East Orlando), since 2022. Born Aguadilla, Puerto Rico; former Orange County School Board member (2018-2022) and 20+ year OCPS teacher (2017 OCPS Teacher of the Year). NOT seeking HD-43 re-election in 2026 -- filed for Orange County Commission District 4.

johanna-lopez@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-43 (D), since 2022 (serving through current term; not seeking HD-43 re-election in 2026, filed for Orange County Commission Dist 4). [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Consistent progressive Democrat. Life: in the Democratic NAY bloc opposing the 2023 6-week ban (SB300, passed 70-40 party-line); NHCSL bio states she 'staunchly opposed' abortion restrictions. Marriage/sexuality: opposed the 2023 trans-bathroom bill HB1521 (passed 80-37 party-line) with floor remarks that it targets an 'imaginary enemy.' Education: 20-year public-school teacher and former school-board member; anti-voucher public-ed champion; endorsed by the Florida Freedom to Read Project (anti-book-restriction). Immigration: in the NAY bloc on SB1718 (2023, 83-36 party-line); endorsed by FLIC Votes. Guns: opposed HB543 permitless carry ('As a teacher, I saw the ways gun violence... impacted young people'); 'Gun Sense Candidate of Distinction' (Moms Demand). No conservative cross-party deviation found.

0.note: In the Democratic NAY bloc opposing the 2023 6-week ban (SB300, 70-40 party-line); NHCSL bio: 'staunchly opposed' abortion restrictions.

1.note: Opposite of abortion abolition: opposed the 6-week ban; pro-choice posture.

0.note: Pro-LGBTQ record; opposed 2023 anti-LGBTQ bills -- does not affirm man-woman-only marriage.

1.note: Opposite of opposing same-sex unions; opposed laws targeting LGBTQ Floridians.

2.note: Opposed the 2023 trans-bathroom bill HB1521 (80-37 party-line); floor remarks that it targets an 'imaginary enemy' and will hurt transgender Floridians.

4.note: Opposed the 2023 anti-LGBTQ legislative package -- opposite of opposing LGBTQ promotion in policy/schools.

0.note: 20-year public-school teacher and former school-board member; anti-voucher public-education champion -- opposite of supporting universal school choice.

2.note: Endorsed by the Florida Freedom to Read Project (anti-book-restriction coalition) -- opposite of removing SOGI/curricula content.
In the Democratic NAY bloc on SB1718 (2023, 83-36 party-line); endorsed by FLIC Votes -- opposite of anti-sanctuary enforcement.

3.note: Opposed SB1718, which included an E-Verify mandate.

1.note: 'Gun Sense Candidate of Distinction' (Moms Demand Action) -- gun-control aligned.

profile.background: Jennifer 'Rita' Harris -- Florida House District 44 (Orlando/Orange County), since 2022; re-elected 2024, up 2026. Secretary of the FL House Climate & Energy Caucus. NOTE: not the Tennessee Sierra Club organizer of the same name.

rita-harris@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-44 (D), since 2022; running for re-election 2026. [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Documented progressive. Life: voted AGAINST the 6-week ban (S300, 2023) and AGAINST the fetal-personhood bill (H289, 2026); sponsored repeal bills (HB1033/HB1283) and the prosecution-shield HB0111; Planned Parenthood PAC endorsement, 'Pro-Choice Champion.' Marriage/sexuality: prime sponsor of HB1235/HB317 abolishing the 'gay and trans panic' legal defense; endorsed by the FL LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus; named by Equality Florida among DeSantis's most vocal critics. Education: pro-public-school-funding campaign, no voucher support. Guns: co-introduced HB1019 (2025 firearm background checks); Everytown endorsement. No conservative cross-party deviation found. (Sourcing note: ChoiceTracker is a pro-choice tracker but its cited bill numbers/dates cross-check against official flsenate.gov/flhouse.gov histories.) Replaces a prior thin speculative 0/100 page.

0.note: Voted AGAINST the 6-week ban (S300, 2023) and AGAINST the fetal-personhood bill (H289, 2026); Planned Parenthood 'Pro-Choice Champion.'

1.note: Sponsored HB1033/HB1283 to repeal FL abortion bans -- opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood PAC (2022 and 2024).

0.note: Endorsed by the FL LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus; pro-LGBTQ record -- does not affirm man-woman-only marriage.

1.note: Among Equality Florida's most vocal critics of the anti-LGBTQ slate -- opposite of opposing same-sex unions.

2.note: Prime sponsor of HB1235/HB317 abolishing the 'gay and trans panic' legal defense -- pro-trans posture.

4.note: Champions LGBTQ inclusion in policy -- opposite of opposing LGBTQ promotion.

0.note: Campaign centers fully funding public schools and raising teacher pay; no voucher/ESA support -- opposite of universal school choice.
Endorsed by Everytown for Gun Safety -- opposite of supporting constitutional carry.

1.note: Co-introduced HB1019 (2025) requiring background checks on firearm sales/transfers.

profile.background: Florida House District 45 (SW Orange / NW Osceola), since Nov 2024. Freshman; the only Democrat who flipped a FL House seat in 2024 and the only House Democrat in a Trump-won district. Running for re-election 2026.

leonard-spencer@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-45 (D), since Nov 2024 (freshman, thin roll-call record). [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Mostly progressive but with one notable pro-police deviation. Life: endorsed by Florida Planned Parenthood PAC (2024); campaign statements pledge to 'protect reproductive rights.' Education: campaign opposes diverting public dollars to vouchers / pro-public-school-funding. PUBLIC JUSTICE DEVIATION (TRUE, self-stated): campaign claims he 'secured increased funding for law enforcement' and backed a DeSantis school-safety initiative -- affirmative pro-police advocacy in his own words (worth a fuller verification on the DeSantis claim). Guns: sponsored HB1107 (2026) granting civil-liability immunity to federal firearms licensees for firearm-hold agreements -- a pro-FFL measure atypical for a Democrat, but its operative mapping to the 2A rubric cells (carry/red-flag/NFA/castle/ATF) is ambiguous, so it is FLAGGED but left unscored. No biblical_marriage evidence found (not on Equality Florida's slate). Most cells null on a genuinely thin freshman record.

0.note: Campaign: 'stand up for the freedom to make personal healthcare decisions' / 'protect reproductive rights' -- pro-choice, does not affirm personhood.

4.note: Endorsed by Florida Planned Parenthood PAC (2024 general).

0.note: Campaign opposes diverting 'public dollars away from neighborhood public schools'; pledges to fund public schools -- opposite of universal school choice.
DEVIATION (self-stated campaign claim): says he 'secured increased funding for law enforcement' and backed a DeSantis school-safety initiative -- affirmative public backing of law enforcement.

profile.background: Jose (Jose) Alvarez -- Florida House District 46 (Osceola/Orange -- Kissimmee/Poinciana), since Nov 2024. Born Havana, Cuba (1964); Kissimmee City Commissioner (2012), Mayor of Kissimmee (2016-2020); former HUD Regional Administrator; Realtor. Running for re-election 2026. (Distinct from Danny Alvarez, R-FL House.)

jose-alvarez@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-46 (D), since Nov 2024 (freshman, thin record). [2026-06 national autopilot refinement, state tier] Two documented, opposite-direction data points and a notable 2A deviation. Marriage: as Kissimmee mayor advanced the city's Domestic Partner Registry among 'inclusive policies' (FALSE on opposing domestic partnerships). 2A DEVIATION (TRUE, highest-value catch): voted YES on HB759 (3/26/2025, passed 78-34) lowering the firearm purchase age from 21 back to 18 -- the ONLY Democrat to vote YES; flagged by name by Equality Florida. Also a separate election deviation (NOT scored -- doesn't map to the 5 election_integrity cells): voted YES with Republicans on HB1205 (2025) restricting the citizen ballot-initiative/petition process. No abortion record located. The 50/100 reflects a genuinely thin 2-cell denominator (1 TRUE gun vote / 1 FALSE partner registry); notes document the deviations and the sparse denominator.

1.note: As Kissimmee mayor advanced the city's Domestic Partner Registry among 'inclusive policies' -- opposite of opposing domestic partnerships.
DEVIATION (HARD): voted YES on HB759 (3/26/2025) lowering the firearm purchase age 21->18, rolling back the post-Parkland restriction -- the ONLY Democrat to vote YES.
